channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Refiner Profits Are Soaring
item.description: U.S. crude refiners are enjoying some of the best profit margins in years.

item.title: Colombia's Oil and Gas Reserves Keep Shrinking
item.description: For a decade, Colombia's economically vital oil industry has been caught in a death spiral. A combination of sharply weaker oil prices, rising geopolitical risk and anti-petroleum industry reforms implemented by…

item.title: Texas Oil Regulator Directs DOGE to Enhance RRC Efficiency
item.description: 'Our goal is simple', RRC Commissioner Wayne Christian said.

item.title: OPEC Oil Production Jumps, But Gulf Supply Is Still Far From Normal
item.description: OPEC's oil production rebounded sharply in June as Gulf producers finally began bringing shut-in barrels back online after months of war-induced disruptions. But despite the impressive headline number, the cartel is…

item.title: China Urges 'Unimpeded Passage' of Hormuz
item.description: China called for the unhindered flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Alberta Advances West Coast Oil Pipeline Project
item.description: Alberta's government said it had applied to list the West Coast Oil Pipeline project, a new gateway for the province's oil sands to British Columbia and eventually Asia, as a project of national interest.

item.title: Copper's Next Move Depends On Washington, Not The Strait Of Hormuz
item.description: Six months after Operation Epic Fury upended the metals complex, the Strait of Hormuz still can't decide if it's open. Days after the two countries signed a memorandum of understanding on June 17, Iran's military…

item.title: Hormuz Turns Oil Outlooks Into a Guessing Game
item.description: Numbers Report – July 03, 2026 In the latest edition of the Numbers Report, we will take a look at some of the most interesting figures put out this week in the energy and metals sectors. Each week we’ll dig into some…

item.title: Which Country is the Biggest Oil Consumer?
item.description: Rigzone examines the Energy Institute's latest statistical review of world energy report, which was released recently.

item.title: Russia Scrambles for Asian Jet Fuel as Crisis Deepens
item.description: Russia's fuel crisis has reached the point where one of the world's biggest oil producers is preparing to import jet fuel. According to Reuters, Russia is set to receive at least 200,000 barrels of jet fuel originating…

item.title: DOJ Urges States to Enforce Fuel Price-Gouging Laws
item.description: The Trump administration is taking its campaign against high gasoline prices to the states, and it’s doing it formally. A little more than a week after President Donald Trump ordered the Justice Department to…

item.title: Oil Markets Grow Numb to U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Drama
item.description: Oil prices remain stuck in a tight range as traders look past geopolitical headlines and wait for clarity on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Friday, July 03, 2026 Following weeks of extremely volatile and…

item.title: Russia Escalates Kyiv Strikes Days Before NATO Summit Showdown
item.description: Russia carried out one of its largest attacks on Ukraine's capital this year, reigniting demands in the United States and Europe for tougher military and economic measures against Moscow.Ukrainian officials said at…

item.title: Woodside Assumes Operatorship of Aussie Gas Assets from ExxonMobil
item.description: The partners continue to each own 50 percent in the Gippsland Basin Joint Venture, which supplies about 40 percent of gas needs on Australia's east coast, and 32.5 percent in the Kipper Unit JV, Woodside said.

item.title: TotalEnergies Offers Millions of Barrels of Iraqi Crude to Asian Buyers
item.description: TotalEnergies is offering millions of barrels of Iraq’s Basrah Medium and Basrah Heavy crudes for prompt delivery to Asia this month and next, traders who had received some of these offers told Bloomberg on Friday…

item.title: ACCC orders phase two review of Saipem-Subsea7 merger
item.description: The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) is set to conduct a detailed Phase 2 review of the proposed merger between Saipem and Subsea7.

item.title: Canada and Alberta advance review of west coast oil pipeline project
item.description: The Government of Canada has referred Alberta's proposed west coast oil pipeline project to the Major Projects Office (MPO) for evaluation as a potential national interest initiative.

item.title: Japan's Top Oil Refiner Eyes a Post-Hormuz Future
item.description: Japan’s biggest oil refiner, Eneos Holdings, will likely move to diversify its crude supply from a heavy dependence on the Middle East in the wake of the shock disruption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz, the…

item.title: ExxonMobil, QatarEnergy Affirm Commerciality of Cyprus Discoveries
item.description: QatarEnergy and ExxonMobil signed a 'commercial discovery declaration' with the Cypriot government for two discoveries in Block 10 in the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Oil Remains in a Divided Market
item.description: Oil remains in a divided market, Waleed Said, Technical Analyst at GivTrade, stated.
